etymology.description: 犬(犬) (quǎn, "dog") + 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + ⺼(⺼) (ròu, "meat") -> dog eating meat with mouth -> satisfaction
etymology.interpretation: The character 猒(yàn) originally depicted a dog eating meat with its mouth, conveying the idea of being satisfied. Over time, the component for mouth transformed into 甘(gān, "sweet") and later into 日(rì, "sun") in the modern form.
